resources.string: To avoid being killed in the background: 1) allow auto-start; 2) add to battery whitelist; 3) allow background pop-ups. Tap OK to open the corresponding settings page.
The next capture will use the full screen. Your selection will be discarded — you can pick a new region anytime.
Floating ball: tap to run the current action; long-press to open the arc menu; drag to move.
A system permission dialog appears on each start
Shizuku installed but not granted; will request on start
Please start the Shizuku app first
Shizuku running but not paired via ADB (no shell privilege). Open the Shizuku app and start it with ADB / wireless debug / root.
Shizuku permission denied; grant it in the Shizuku app and retry
Shizuku is not ready. Grant permission in Shizuku and make sure it was started with ADB / wireless debugging / root.
Retries once with the same engine when the first response is empty. This may add one API request and additional cost.
Tip: download det.onnx / rec.onnx plus keys.txt for v5 or inference.yml for v6, then tap \"Import from device files\" and pick all three at once.
Invert colors (for dark bg with light text)
Start with Shizuku (skip per-launch system dialog)
Shizuku running but not granted (tap Start to request)
Shizuku not running (start it via ADB / wireless debugging)
Shizuku not installed (optional; default MediaProjection works)
Save your changes before going back?
Third-party notices for dependencies and model assets included in or downloaded by this app
The bundled license notices could not be loaded.
Takes effect immediately. You can also change it in system Settings → Apps → Language.
Image limits: longest side ≤ 4096px / shortest side ≥ 15px, aspect ratio 1:4–4:1, base64 < 4MB. The 2× upscale preprocess doubles image size — narrow or huge capture regions easily hit error 216202.
Only private/loopback addresses are allowed over cleartext by default. Hosts added here also permit http:// access. ⚠ Cleartext can be intercepted/tampered — only enable when the link is trusted.
Volcengine console → Access keys → AccessKey ID
Secret Access Key of the same key pair
Enable \"Machine Translation\" in the Volcengine console, then issue an access key. Region currently fixed to cn-north-1. Supports native batch (TextList).
Secret key for the same APPID
Baidu Translate Open Platform (fanyi-api.baidu.com) — **not** Baidu AI Cloud OCR. Free tier: 1 QPS / 50k chars per month. Sign up at fanyi-api.baidu.com.

Shares SecretId / SecretKey / Region with Tencent Cloud OCR — edits here sync to the OCR section. Default ap-guangzhou. Native batch via TextTranslateBatch (≤ 2000 chars / call). First-time setup requires opening Machine Translation at console.cloud.tencent.com/tmt.
No config needed. Google may rate-limit or change the endpoint any time — for learning only. Proxy required inside mainland China.
Translation runs entirely on the device after the language model is downloaded. A 30 MB language model is required. No API key is needed.
The current language models are ready. Offline translation is available.

The on-device models required for %1$s → %2$s have not all been downloaded. Missing: %3$s.\n\nModels are reusable per language. After downloading, the language can be translated offline in either direction with other ready languages.
App Secret of the same application
Generic OCR — billed per character; trial quota available on the console.
End-to-end engine: sends the full image and returns translated regions in one call — **bypasses the OCR engine above**. Billed per call.
Free plan ends with :fx; Pro does not
Tip: Free plan gives 500k chars/month — sign up at https://www.deepl.com/pro-api. DeepL doesn\'t stream; the full result arrives at once. Chinese (Traditional) requires Pro.
Root URL only, e.g. http://host:port/ — no /translate suffix
Custom URL overrides the Pro toggle. Test connection then probes via a real translate call. http:// only allowed for private/loopback addresses (LAN, localhost); use https:// for public hosts.
Uses DeepL official v2 endpoints (free/pro) with API Key above. Base URL / Custom Token / Bearer are all ignored.
Uses deeplx protocol: endpoint = Base URL + /translate, auth via Custom Token + Bearer toggle. Base URL is required.
Tries deeplx first; falls back to DeepL official when deeplx fails or returns empty. Requires both Base URL (deeplx) and API Key (official fallback).

**Fully isolated from the DeepL API Key above** — switching to a custom URL will never send your official key to third parties. Put your self-hosted service\'s access token here; leave blank if it has no auth.
Custom URL only. Default uses DeepL-Auth-Key (official). Most deeplx deployments use Bearer. Reads the Custom Endpoint Token field above — your official DeepL key is never touched.
Shows speaker buttons after source and translated text in word-select translation. Speech starts only when a speaker button is tapped.
Uses voices exposed by the current Android TextToSpeech engine. Emotion is ignored; change or install engines in system settings.
Sends JSON to Base URL + /api/tts for a local PC service or custom gateway.
Uses the official unidirectional streaming HTTP API. Preset and cloned voices require different Resource IDs; voice management uses a separate AK/SK-signed API.
Uses the synchronous t2a_v2 HTTP API. Select the API site that owns the key and plan; cloned or designed voice_id values are supported.
Search language, voice name, or Voice ID
No activated cloned or designed voices were returned.
Enter the MiniMax API Key before using account voice management.
Confirm using “%1$s”? Its language-specific sample will also replace the TTS test text.
Voice previews call the MiniMax speech synthesis API and incur usage charges.
Tap a voice to fill its Voice ID.
MP3, M4A, or WAV; 10 seconds to 5 minutes; at most 20 MB. MiniMax requires a verified personal or business account.
8–256 characters; start with a letter; use letters, numbers, - or _.
Optional MP3, M4A, or WAV shorter than 8 seconds; requires its exact transcript.
The transcript must end with punctuation.
Source audio transcript for ASR validation (optional)
Confirm to call MiniMax and create the voice. This operation may incur charges.
Voice created and set as the current voice. It will sync to account voices after the first successful speech synthesis.
Voice created and set as the current voice, but MiniMax did not return preview audio.
Describe age, gender, timbre, accent, and delivery.
Uses the OpenAI-compatible Base URL, API Key, and model configured under Translation to refine the current non-empty voice description. This does not create or charge for a MiniMax preview.
Required, up to 500 characters. MiniMax charges for synthesizing this preview.
Plays the preview audio already returned by MiniMax without another synthesis request.
%1$s will be permanently deleted and cannot be used again.
Select an MP3, M4A, or WAV audio file.
Source audio must be at least 10 seconds.
Source audio must not exceed 5 minutes.
Prompt audio must be shorter than 8 seconds.
Voice ID must contain at least 8 characters.
Voice ID must not exceed 256 characters.
Voice ID must start with an English letter.
Voice ID may contain only English letters, numbers, - and _.
Voice ID must not end with - or _.
Uses the MiMo V2.5 Chat Completions API. Style and emotion use natural-language instructions.
e.g. zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural / longwan / installed voice name
The current TTS engine did not expose any voices. Install voice data in system settings, then refresh.
Speech tests call the selected TTS service and may incur usage charges.
Hello, this is a text-to-speech test.
Enter text to synthesize with the current settings
The TTS test request was accepted.
MiMo officially restricts Token Plan to coding tools and prohibits non-coding app backend/API use. For this TTS feature, use the pay-as-you-go endpoint unless MiMo authorizes your scenario.
https://api.example.com or a complete endpoint URL
Enter a valid HTTP or HTTPS Base URL.
For example: Speak in a warm, restrained and confident tone
Only for voice clone 2.0 with synthesis text in the same language as the training audio.
Applies only to online TTS audio played by this app (Generic HTTP, Volcengine, MiniMax, and MiMo). Android system TTS bypasses the app player. High gain may cause heavy compression or distortion.
Describe age, gender, timbre, texture, accent, or persona
Describe delivery, emotion, pace, transitions, emphasis, role, or scene
Uses the OpenAI-compatible Base URL, API Key, and model configured under Translation to refine the current non-empty voice description.
Enter a voice description before using AI generation.
Enter a rough style intent before using AI polish.

Natural-language control (enter it in this field)
This content is sent as role=user and is not spoken. One instruction can direct style transitions, mixed emotions, and paragraph-, sentence-, word-, or character-level performance.

For detailed acting, use director mode:
Role: identity, personality, appearance, and speaking habits.
Scene: what is happening, who is listening, and the emotional position.
Direction: pace, breath, pauses, emphasis, resonance, vocal texture, and emotional movement.

Example: Speak brightly and energetically, slightly faster than normal, with rising intonation and barely contained excitement when announcing good news.

Audio-label control (put it in the text to be spoken, not this field)
Overall style labels go at the start of role=assistant text, for example: (melancholy)It has been years since I walked down this street. Multiple labels may share (), （）, or []. Inline audio labels such as [whisper], [sigh], [sob], [laugh], or [speed up] may appear inside the spoken text.

Common directions include happy, sad, angry, calm, gentle, lively, serious, magnetic, clear, hoarse, dialects, role-play, breathing, trembling, crying, and laughter. Singing is supported only by the preset-voice model; put (singing) at the very start and Chinese lyrics generally work best.

AI polish uses the OpenAI-compatible Base URL, API Key, and model configured under Translation. Enter a rough non-empty intent first; AI converts it into a natural-language role=user instruction and does not generate audio labels.
Official limit: MP3/WAV only; base64-encoded sample must not exceed 10 MB.
OK · fetched %1$d models — tap to fill model name
check Free/Pro endpoint toggle matches your key
Pick a model from the list
Current prompt doesn\'t use the %1$s placeholder; the prompt text won\'t follow language changes.
Note: a hidden rule still forces the target language to \"%1$s\", so the output language stays correct.
On-device (no network, models downloaded on demand)
Cloud (requires API key, pay per call)
"Youdao PicTrans" end-to-end engine is active — it does OCR + translation in one call, so the OCR settings below are ignored.
Run Umi-OCR on the PC, enable its HTTP service, and use the PC LAN IP here. The app sends PNG screenshots and expects dict output with text/score/box.
Run LunaTranslator on the PC, enable its API service, and use the PC LAN IP here. The app sends PNG screenshots as the image field and reads results/text/box from the response.
PP-OCRv6 Online uses the PaddleOCR AI Studio asynchronous OCR service. Get an Access Token from the page below, then enter it here.
Open PaddleOCR AI Studio in an external browser
No browser is available to open this link.

Pick the OCR engine for the text type:
• Auto / Japanese / Korean / Chinese / Latin: Google ML Kit on-device (free, offline). "Auto" picks the model by detecting Hangul / kana; pinning a specific language slightly improves accuracy.
• PaddleOCR: on-device multilingual; better on complex layouts. First use downloads ~20MB models. (Korean dict not bundled — use ML Kit or cloud for Korean.)
• Manga-OCR (Japanese manga, on-device, ≈ 160 MB; encoder + decoder + tokenizer files)
• Baidu / Tencent Cloud: cloud OCR with API key (pay-as-you-go); pick Korean / Japanese etc. under "Recognition language" as a fallback.
Endpoint type (each has its own free quota on the Baidu console)
\nWith location: overlay snaps to each text box
\nWithout location: text stacks vertically on the left of the screen
Action type (each has its own quota on Tencent Console)

2× upscale enlarges the screenshot before OCR and can help tiny on-device text. Avoid it with cloud OCR: returned text boxes may be based on the enlarged image, so translated overlays can shift on the original screenshot. It also increases upload size.
Avoid 2× upscale with cloud OCR; translated overlay positions may shift.
Invert colors (for dark bg with light text)
For blocks stuck to source text only: derive colors, background, and text size from each text region.
When enabled, placement is fixed to overlap with zero offsets, wrapping enabled, and collision avoidance disabled. Turning it off restores the saved layout.
Long-press an imported font to delete it.
Delete “%1$s”? If it is currently selected, translation text will return to the system default font.
The selected font file is empty.
The selected font is too large. Use a file under 50 MB.
Could not read the selected font file.
Android could not load this font. Try another .ttf file.
Could not save the font file.
Hello, world.\nThe weather is lovely today.
Vertical translation blocks do not support direct long-press text selection. To copy vertical text, use Tap to select and copy it from the selectable text panel.
Long press a translation block to show the system selection handles and copy any range. Tap still closes the translation layer.
Tap a translation block to open a panel where you can select part of the text or copy the full source or translation.
When locked, the floating window can\'t be dragged or resized. Useful to avoid mis-touch during games.
Allow text wrap (off = force single line)
Avoid covering other text (4-direction collision detection)
With collision detection: the overlay won\'t intrude into the next text box on the right; wrapped lines shrink in height to fit the box below.
Merge adjacent boxes in same row (avoid fragmentation overlap)

In comics / subtitles, OCR often splits one sentence into adjacent small boxes (\"WHEN SHE | SOBERS UP. | THANK\"), causing overlays to overlap. When enabled, these are merged into one box before translation, eliminating the overlap at the source.
Strict thresholds: rarely over-merges but paragraphs may stay split. Suits visual novels / dense long passages.
Loose thresholds: merges multi-line bubbles more readily. Recommended for comics / short bubbles; may merge adjacent bubbles by accident.
Typing resets the timer. A stable frame translates immediately; on-device OCR can also confirm unchanged text over animated backgrounds. Cloud OCR uses frame stability only to avoid extra requests.
Auto balances text size and position. Lower screen first suits game dialogue boxes. Anywhere does not prefer a screen area.
When off, the dialogue region still controls stability, but all text recognized in the first frame is translated.
Higher values detect smaller changes. Exact matches skip OCR and translation; similar frames still confirm that OCR text is unchanged.
Loop trigger mode and text stability
Hold Volume+ and Volume- together for 0.3s to trigger one capture. Single key still adjusts volume normally.
Shows diagnostic controls for OCR and translation troubleshooting.
Saves the screenshots used by OCR and attaches them to runtime logs. Limited to 8 screenshots per app process.
Always performs a real translation request for performance testing, without reading or writing the in-memory translation cache.
Draws each OCR bounding box at its detected screen coordinates.
When translation is off, capture stops after OCR and does not call the translator.
Applies to all cloud APIs: Baidu / Tencent OCR, OpenAI-compatible translation, DeepL. Streaming translation uses 2× this limit.\nPaddleOCR on-device model download is not affected (uses a fixed 60s).
Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, English, Japanese

Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, English, French, German, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese, Dutch, Polish, Romanian, Czech, Swedish, Norwegian, Danish, Finnish, Hungarian, Turkish, Vietnamese, Indonesian, Malay, Azerbaijani, Afrikaans, Bosnian, Croatian, Welsh, Estonian, Irish, Icelandic, Kurdish, Lithuanian, Latvian, Maltese, Maori, Occitan, Slovak, Slovenian, Albanian, Swahili, Tagalog, Uzbek, Latin, Serbian (Latin), Catalan, Basque, Galician, Luxembourgish, Romansh, Quechua

Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, English, Japanese, French, German, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese, Dutch, Polish, Romanian, Czech, Swedish, Norwegian, Danish, Finnish, Hungarian, Turkish, Vietnamese, Indonesian, Malay, Azerbaijani, Afrikaans, Bosnian, Croatian, Welsh, Estonian, Irish, Icelandic, Kurdish, Lithuanian, Latvian, Maltese, Maori, Occitan, Slovak, Slovenian, Albanian, Swahili, Tagalog, Uzbek, Latin, Serbian (Latin), Catalan, Basque, Galician, Luxembourgish, Romansh, Quechua
PaddleOCR on-device model. Choose v5 mobile, v6 tiny, v6 small, or v6 medium. Try \"Auto download\" first; if it fails, import det.onnx / rec.onnx plus keys.txt for v5 or inference.yml for v6.
Import / export settings, presets, and fonts
Bold, italic, spacing, outline and shadow
DeepL advanced (custom URL / Bearer / protocol / DeepLX)
Border style (solid / dashed / dotted / double / groove)
PaddleOCR not downloaded. Use Auto download, or import local model files.

On-device manga-ocr for Japanese manga (≈ 160 MB; encoder + decoder + tokenizer files). Reuses PaddleOCR DBNet for detection — please download PaddleOCR model too. Source: huggingface.co — VPN / proxy required.
Manga OCR not downloaded. Use Auto download with the Network download source, or import local files.
No logs yet — they show up here once the capture service runs.
No logs match the current filter
OCR screenshot used for this log entry
Choose what to include in the shared export.
Include OCR screenshots and export a ZIP
No OCR screenshots in the current log filter
Log was too large for the clipboard, so a summary was copied. Use share to save the full log.
Auto translate on (wait for text to remain stable for %1$d ms)
Capture failed: no frame. Try restarting capture.
Shizuku capture isn\'t working — likely not paired via ADB. Open the Shizuku app and start it with ADB / wireless debug, or switch to MediaProjection on the main screen.
OCR result looks unreliable. Adjust the capture region or try another OCR engine.
Loop mode selected. Tap the floating ball to start.
Double tap to translate. Long press to open the menu
Double tap to select text. Long press to open the menu
Double tap to start looping. Long press to open the menu
Double tap to stop looping. Long press to open the menu
After release, springs to the nearest edge and tucks 1/3 off-screen (idle, semi-transparent). Wakes up when pressed. Off: stays at release position with no tuck.
After the long-press menu closes, dock to the nearest edge if no further touch within 3s. Only effective when “Snap to edge” is on.
Dock inset from physical edge: %1$d dp
0 = flush against the system edge. Raise this to avoid full-gesture edge trigger zones.
Capture failed: no frame (may need to re-authorize / Shizuku unavailable)
Shizuku dry-run failed at service start — likely not paired via ADB. Stopped capture service.
Tap the floating ball to run its current mode · long-press for menu

Listens for a volume-key combo (Volume+ and Volume- held together for 0.3s) as a global shortcut to trigger screen translation. Only active after you turn it on in settings; does not read screen content or window structure.
The \"Web image\" endpoint ignores language_type (always uses Chinese-English). To recognize Korean / Japanese etc., switch to Standard / Accurate / Standard+location / Accurate+location.
Accurate endpoint only supports Chinese / Auto; other choices fall back to Auto.
OCR engine may not recognize this
Source language is \"%1$s\"; the current OCR engine may not recognize it well.\nRecommend switching to \"%2$s\".
Source language is \"%1$s\"; the current \"%2$s\" engine\'s recognition language is \"%3$s\", which may not recognize it well.\nRecommend \"%4$s\" — tap \"Apply\" to adjust (engine will not change).
\"%1$s\" recognition language is \"%2$s\", but the source language is \"%3$s\".\nRecommend changing the source language to \"%4$s\" — tap \"Apply\" to adjust.
For \"%1$s\", \"%2$s\" (cloud OCR) is recommended. Add the matching API key in the OCR settings.
%1$s\n\nApi.github.com may be unreachable from some networks; tap below to open the Release page manually.
LLM-enhanced, good for complex layout / handwriting
Drag to select the translation area · double-tap for full screen
Baidu OCR: API Key / Secret Key not configured
Tencent OCR: SecretId / SecretKey not configured
PaddleOCR AI Studio token not configured
PaddleOCR model not ready. Tap \"Download PaddleOCR model\" in Settings.
Manga OCR model not ready. Tap \"Download Manga OCR model\" in Settings.
%1$s failed: %2$s. huggingface.co unreachable — please enable proxy / VPN, or use \"Import from device files\".
%1$s model not ready. Tap \"Download model\" in Settings → On-device LLM translator.
On-device LLM translator requires Android 13+. Your device is not supported.
Runs Hy-MT2 / Sakura locally. Requires Android 13+ and ≥ 8 GB RAM. Models are downloaded on demand (Wi-Fi recommended).
Downloaded files will be removed from this device. You\'ll need to download or import them again before next use.
The phone is currently using mobile data. Downloading %1$s may use your data plan.
The app could not confirm that the phone is on Wi-Fi. Downloading %1$s may use mobile data.
Translation powered by Tencent Hy-MT2 and SakuraLLM. See license notices in About.
Custom model mirror URL (base, with trailing /)
Sakura supports Japanese to Simplified Chinese only
Current source language is \"%1$s\". Sakura only supports Japanese source and Simplified Chinese target.
Current target language is \"%1$s\". Sakura outputs Simplified Chinese only.
Current language pair is \"%1$s\" → \"%2$s\". Sakura only supports Japanese source and Simplified Chinese target.

%1$s

This setting will automatically fall back to OpenAI-compatible LLM for unsupported Sakura language pairs.

Fallback configuration is present. Use "Test connection" if you want to verify it before saving.
%1$s\n\nFallback is not ready. Missing: %2$s. Configure OpenAI-compatible LLM, or switch to Sakura\'s supported language pair.
960 px maximum side, one detection pass. The default and fastest option; tiny or faint text may be missed.
1920 px maximum side plus overlapping tiled detection and deduplication for large images. Prioritizes tiny, faint, and narrow vertical text at a higher runtime cost.
Lower thresholds catch smaller / fainter text but may add noise boxes. Take effect on next capture.
This will reset DBNet thresholds, unclip ratio, bubble cluster gap, and Manga crop padding for the current OCR engine. The change takes effect on the next capture.
PaddleOCR calls this `thresh`: pixels in the output probability map above this score are treated as text pixels. Lower values keep faint or tiny strokes; too low can turn background noise into boxes.
PaddleOCR calls this `box_thresh`: a candidate box is kept only when the average score of pixels inside it is above this value. Lower values keep weak text regions; too low keeps false boxes.

PaddleOCR calls this `unclip_ratio`: the expansion ratio used by the Vatti clipping step to enlarge the detected text region. Larger values give recognition more margin; too large may swallow neighboring text.

App-specific manga tuning, not a PaddleOCR official parameter: nearby DBNet boxes are expanded by this many pixels before bubble clustering. Larger values merge adjacent vertical columns more aggressively; too large may merge separate bubbles.
Adds margin only to the Manga OCR recognition crop; it does not enlarge red boxes or translation layout. Keep 0 when DBNet unclip is sufficient, and increase only if edge characters are clipped.
On-device LLM (no network, model downloaded on demand)
Tap the ball to translate full screen
Tap the ball to drag a selection
Drag a rectangle around text to translate
No text recognized in the selected area
Translation: %1$s. Tap to close. Long press to select text and open the system copy menu.
Translation: %1$s. Double tap to open the selectable copy panel.
Screen Translator needs the \"Display over other apps\" permission
Speech failed: the system voice engine does not support %1$s. Change the speech language or install its voice data.
Choose 2-6 visible buttons. When there are more actions, the last slot becomes Next page.
Drag to reorder. Up to %1$d buttons show per page; when there is another page, the last slot becomes Next page.
Presets apply OCR, translator, language direction, TTS state/provider, render style, and related tuning. API keys and tokens are never saved in presets.
Another preset is downloading. Wait for it to finish before downloading this one.
Missing required model(s): %1$s. Download or import them before applying this preset.
on-device LLM unsupported on this device
Delete “%1$s”? This only removes the custom preset. It does not delete models or API keys.
A preset with this name already exists.
Current settings match an existing preset.
The selected file contains no custom presets.
Import %1$d preset(s). Presets replaced by name: %2$s
Exported portable settings, %1$d custom preset(s), %2$d font(s), and %3$d terminology item(s). Credentials, private endpoints, allowlisted hosts, and device geometry were not included.

This imports %1$d preset(s); presets replaced by name: %2$s. Font files: %3$d. Terminology items: %4$d. Incompatible setting fields to skip: %5$d. This device keeps %6$d protected credential, private-connection, runtime, and geometry fields.
Imported settings, %1$d preset(s), %3$d font(s), and %4$d terminology item(s); replaced %2$d preset(s) and skipped %5$d incompatible setting field(s).

When the selected region looks like a single word or fixed phrase, the LLM returns structured pronunciation, definitions, difficult usage or terminology notes, and examples. Non-OpenAI engines fall back to plain translation.
Your current custom prompt will be overwritten by the default for this UI language. This can\'t be undone.

This mainly improves non-horizontal text, such as vertical layouts. It detects layout direction from OCR bbox geometry and can re-run OCR with a better engine when needed. If you do not need it, delete the orientation model package to reduce storage use and turn this off to improve translation speed.
Manual lock active — auto detection is bypassed.

Optional PaddleOCR orientation pack (~7.5 MB): whole-image 0/90/180/270 classification plus text-line 0/180 classification. It can route vertical screens before OCR and rerun upside-down horizontal text once with boxes mapped back.
Orientation models incomplete (post-OCR geometry fallback still works)
Orientation model not ready. Download or import the ONNX model in Settings.
%1$s failed: %2$s. Check network/proxy, or use local import.
Use the recognized horizontal or vertical layout and reading order. Turn this off to choose both manually.
Use global and per-app names and terminology during LLM translation
This translation engine cannot receive app names or terminology. These options currently apply to OpenAI-compatible and on-device LLM translation.
Send app name to the model
The package name stays on device; this option sends only the display name
Search source, translation, app, or category
No entries match the current filters
An entry with the same scope, language pair, source term, and case rule already exists: “%1$s -> %2$s”. Replace its translation with “%3$s”?
